headline: Public Complaints Bureau resolved 34% of cases — 52,000 citizens ignored
context: The Public Complaints Bureau resolved only 34% of 78,000 complaints received in 2025, leaving 52,000 citizens without resolution or formal response to their grievances.

cards.big: 52,000 public complaints went unresolved in 2025 — 66% simply ignored
cards.sub: Government's own complaint mechanism fails two-thirds of citizens who use it

cards.big: PCB operates under PM's Department with no statutory enforcement authority
cards.sub: Bureau can only forward complaints to offending agencies — cannot compel action

cards.big: PCB budget of RM18 million serves 78,000 complainants — RM231 per case
cards.sub: Chronic underfunding makes meaningful complaint resolution impossible

cards.big: Complaints about the same 8 agencies recur year after year with no systemic fix
cards.sub: Pattern indicates structural governance failures, not individual service lapses

cards.big: A complaint system with 34% resolution rate teaches citizens that complaining is pointless
Should PCB be replaced with a statutory ombudsman with binding resolution powers?
